Activity.description: Hyperedge. Consumes input grits, applies an interpretation under stated assumptions, emits output grits. Records a transform step in the hyperDAG topology.

admissibility_rationale.description: Why this Activity is valid given its inputs and viewpoint.

generation_mode.description: Free-form descriptor of the process that generated this grit (parser name + version, viewpoint name + version, LLM model + tier).

inputs.description: Input grit IDs consumed by this Activity.

outputs.description: New grits produced by this Activity. May be empty for declarative edges (SUPPORT, CONTRADICTION) whose output is the Activity itself.

provenance.description: Provenance description for v1. Future versions will model provenance as structured edges into the hyperDAG; for now a free-form string is accepted to allow ingestion bundles from upstream extraction tools.

scope.description: Optional but recommended. Viewpoint-supplied scope dimensions describing the conditions under which this grit's statements apply. The core Scope marker carries no domain dimensions; load a viewpoint schema to populate them.

should_not_claim.description: Epistemic boundaries this grit must respect. Combination of per-class defaults plus directive-imposed rules from the viewpoint.

type.description: For Object and EvidenceRecord, a CURIE into a viewpoint vocabulary. For Activity, a CURIE corresponding to the ActivityType value (e.g. grits:activity_type/synthesis_edge).

viewpoint_directive_id.description: Reference to the ViewpointDirective that shaped this grit. The bootstrap meta-viewpoint and the blank-slate viewpoint are valid references; absence is not.

Activity.title: Activity

ActivityType.description: Structural type of an Activity (hyperedge in the topology).
ActivityType.title: ActivityType

BboxLocator.description: Axis-aligned bounding box on a rasterized page.

locator_type.description: Class name of the concrete Locator subclass (e.g. CharRangeLocator).

BboxLocator.title: BboxLocator

CharRangeLocator.description: Character range within extracted text of a source artifact.

locator_type.description: Class name of the concrete Locator subclass (e.g. CharRangeLocator).

CharRangeLocator.title: CharRangeLocator

CompatibilityJudgment.description: A recorded compatibility judgment over a set of grits/evidence.
CompatibilityJudgment.title: CompatibilityJudgment

Composable.description: Mixin granting a layer a composition_mode. Parent references are NOT in this mixin — each composable class declares its own semantically explicit parent slot (parent_viewpoint_ids, parent_extraction_profile_ids, etc.).

composition_mode.description: How this layer folds against its declared parents during resolution. Defaults to additive.

Composable.title: Composable

ComposedViewpointDirective.description: The frozen result of composing a ViewpointDirective with optional ExtractionProfile, VocabularyPack, and ReasoningPolicy layers. Still a viewpoint-level interpretive contract (and still hashed by the one canonical pipeline), it inlines the resolved operational layers and records the flattened source chains for inspectability. Produced by compose_viewpoint(); not hand-authored.

composition_mode.description: How this layer folds against its declared parents during resolution. Defaults to additive.

directive_name.description: Human-readable name (e.g. viewpoint:materials_science:v1). Combined with content hash, this gives identity-by-declaration.

evidence_record_ids.description: References to EvidenceRecord grits anchoring this Object's claims.

features.description: Viewpoint-defined structured payload, serialized as a JSON string in v1. The viewpoint's vocabulary determines the shape. Later versions may use a typed Any with viewpoint-declared schemas.

generation_mode.description: Free-form descriptor of the process that generated this grit (parser name + version, viewpoint name + version, LLM model + tier).

imposed_should_not_claim.description: should_not_claim rules this directive imposes on every grit extracted under it. Combined with per-class defaults at extraction time.

operation_link_ids.description: Backward pointers to ACTION_EDGE Activities involving this Object.

parent_viewpoint_ids.description: ViewpointDirective ids this directive composes from, parents-first. Resolution is explicit (the caller supplies the registry); there is no implicit runtime parent lookup.

provenance.description: Provenance description for v1. Future versions will model provenance as structured edges into the hyperDAG; for now a free-form string is accepted to allow ingestion bundles from upstream extraction tools.

resolved_extraction_profile.description: The resolved extraction profile, if one was composed.

resolved_reasoning_policy.description: The resolved reasoning policy, if one was composed.

resolved_vocabulary_pack.description: The resolved vocabulary pack, if one was composed.

scope.description: Optional but recommended. Viewpoint-supplied scope dimensions describing the conditions under which this grit's statements apply. The core Scope marker carries no domain dimensions; load a viewpoint schema to populate them.

should_not_claim.description: Epistemic boundaries this grit must respect. Combination of per-class defaults plus directive-imposed rules from the viewpoint.

source_artifact_refs.description: ContentReferences to the source artifacts this Object derives from.

source_extraction_profile_ids.description: Flattened, parents-first chain of extraction profile ids that were composed.

source_reasoning_policy_ids.description: Flattened, parents-first chain of reasoning policy ids that were composed.

source_viewpoint_ids.description: Flattened, parents-first chain of viewpoint directive ids that were composed.

source_vocabulary_pack_ids.description: Flattened, parents-first chain of vocabulary pack ids that were composed.

synthesis_link_ids.description: Backward pointers to Activities that referenced this Object as input or output.

target_schema.description: Reference to the LinkML schema (or schema profile) this directive commits to.

type.description: For Object and EvidenceRecord, a CURIE into a viewpoint vocabulary. For Activity, a CURIE corresponding to the ActivityType value (e.g. grits:activity_type/synthesis_edge).

unspecified_items.description: Dimensions or claims not bound under the current viewpoint.

viewpoint_directive_id.description: Reference to the ViewpointDirective that shaped this grit. The bootstrap meta-viewpoint and the blank-slate viewpoint are valid references; absence is not.

ComposedViewpointDirective.title: ComposedViewpointDirective

CompositeLocator.description: A locator that combines multiple sub-locators.

locator_type.description: Class name of the concrete Locator subclass (e.g. CharRangeLocator).

CompositeLocator.title: CompositeLocator

CompositionMode.description: How a composable layer folds against its declared parents during deterministic resolution. Selected per layer; not every layer type supports every mode.
CompositionMode.title: CompositionMode

Confidence.description: Structured confidence carrying calibration metadata. A calibrated confidence outside its calibration_domain or under a different viewpoint is no longer calibrated — downstream consumers must downgrade to heuristic.

calibration_scope.description: The data distribution under which the confidence was calibrated.

confidence_domain.description: The input domain where the calibration applies.

value.description: Numeric confidence; semantics depend on confidence_basis.

viewpoint_directive_id.description: Viewpoint under which calibration was performed.

Confidence.title: Confidence

ConfidenceBasis.description: Source semantics of a confidence value.
ConfidenceBasis.title: ConfidenceBasis

ContentReference.description: Content-addressed reference to externally stored content. Identity by uri + sha256 + hash_mode. At resolution time, the system fetches uri, recomputes the hash under hash_mode, and refuses to use the content if the hash does not match.

uri.description: Locator (file path, https URL, did:..., cid:...).

ContentReference.title: ContentReference

EpistemicStatus.description: Epistemic label on a consequential statement.
EpistemicStatus.title: EpistemicStatus

EvidenceDensity.description: How densely an extraction profile expects claims to be grounded in evidence.
EvidenceDensity.title: EvidenceDensity

EvidenceRecord.description: Anchor unit. Grounded data anchored to a single source artifact via a typed locator. Referenced by Objects' evidence_record_ids and by Activities' inputs.

cited_from.description: Identifier or marker for prior evidence this record derives from or cites. May be `unknown_external` when citation resolution has not happened.

evidence_type.description: CURIE identifying the kind of scientific content the locator anchors. No core-supplied permissible values; viewpoints supply the evidence-type vocabulary they use.

generation_mode.description: Free-form descriptor of the process that generated this grit (parser name + version, viewpoint name + version, LLM model + tier).

normalized_payload.description: Viewpoint-defined structured payload, serialized as a JSON string in v1.

provenance.description: Provenance description for v1. Future versions will model provenance as structured edges into the hyperDAG; for now a free-form string is accepted to allow ingestion bundles from upstream extraction tools.

scope.description: Optional but recommended. Viewpoint-supplied scope dimensions describing the conditions under which this grit's statements apply. The core Scope marker carries no domain dimensions; load a viewpoint schema to populate them.

should_not_claim.description: Epistemic boundaries this grit must respect. Combination of per-class defaults plus directive-imposed rules from the viewpoint.

source_artifact_ref.description: Single source artifact this evidence is extracted from.

type.description: For Object and EvidenceRecord, a CURIE into a viewpoint vocabulary. For Activity, a CURIE corresponding to the ActivityType value (e.g. grits:activity_type/synthesis_edge).

viewpoint_directive_id.description: Reference to the ViewpointDirective that shaped this grit. The bootstrap meta-viewpoint and the blank-slate viewpoint are valid references; absence is not.

EvidenceRecord.title: EvidenceRecord

ExtractionGranularity.description: How finely an extraction profile expects content to be decomposed.
ExtractionGranularity.title: ExtractionGranularity

ExtractionProfile.description: Extraction/grounding semantics: how finely content is decomposed, how densely claims are grounded, and what locator fidelity is expected. Core understands only abstract content kinds identified by CURIEs; the semantic meaning of those CURIEs is supplied by viewpoint vocabularies. This layer carries no infrastructure settings (no retry, OCR, chunking, parallelism, transport, or cache concerns). Supported composition modes: additive, overriding.

composition_mode.description: How this layer folds against its declared parents during resolution. Defaults to additive.

evidence_density.description: How densely claims should be grounded in evidence.

evidence_record_ids.description: References to EvidenceRecord grits anchoring this Object's claims.

features.description: Viewpoint-defined structured payload, serialized as a JSON string in v1. The viewpoint's vocabulary determines the shape. Later versions may use a typed Any with viewpoint-declared schemas.

generation_mode.description: Free-form descriptor of the process that generated this grit (parser name + version, viewpoint name + version, LLM model + tier).

granularity.description: How finely content should be decomposed during extraction.

high_fidelity_content_kinds.description: Content kinds (viewpoint-supplied CURIEs) requiring high-fidelity locators and verbatim preservation.

layer_name.description: Human-readable name (e.g. extraction_profile:detailed:v0). Combined with content hash, this gives identity-by-declaration.

locator_fidelity.description: Expected locator granularity for anchored content.

operation_link_ids.description: Backward pointers to ACTION_EDGE Activities involving this Object.

parent_extraction_profile_ids.description: ExtractionProfile ids this profile composes from, parents-first.

preserve_content_kinds.description: Content kinds (viewpoint-supplied CURIEs) whose content must be preserved. Core assigns no meaning to these CURIEs.

preserve_numeric_values.description: Whether reported numeric values must be preserved verbatim.

preserve_uncertainty_language.description: Whether uncertainty/hedging language must be preserved verbatim.

provenance.description: Provenance description for v1. Future versions will model provenance as structured edges into the hyperDAG; for now a free-form string is accepted to allow ingestion bundles from upstream extraction tools.

require_char_level_locators.description: Whether anchored content requires character-level locators.

require_grounding_for_content_kinds.description: Content kinds (viewpoint-supplied CURIEs) that must be grounded in an evidence record.

scope.description: Optional but recommended. Viewpoint-supplied scope dimensions describing the conditions under which this grit's statements apply. The core Scope marker carries no domain dimensions; load a viewpoint schema to populate them.

should_not_claim.description: Epistemic boundaries this grit must respect. Combination of per-class defaults plus directive-imposed rules from the viewpoint.

source_artifact_refs.description: ContentReferences to the source artifacts this Object derives from.

synthesis_link_ids.description: Backward pointers to Activities that referenced this Object as input or output.

viewpoint_directive_id.description: Reference to the ViewpointDirective that shaped this grit. The bootstrap meta-viewpoint and the blank-slate viewpoint are valid references; absence is not.

Grit.description: Abstract base for all pygrits graph nodes. Carries the discipline contract: identity, type, viewpoint, provenance, lifecycle, review state, should_not_claim, scope, generation mode. Three concrete role subclasses: Object, Activity, EvidenceRecord.

generation_mode.description: Free-form descriptor of the process that generated this grit (parser name + version, viewpoint name + version, LLM model + tier).

provenance.description: Provenance description for v1. Future versions will model provenance as structured edges into the hyperDAG; for now a free-form string is accepted to allow ingestion bundles from upstream extraction tools.

scope.description: Optional but recommended. Viewpoint-supplied scope dimensions describing the conditions under which this grit's statements apply. The core Scope marker carries no domain dimensions; load a viewpoint schema to populate them.

HashMode.description: How a ContentReference's sha256 is computed.

LineageType.description: Independence classification of an evidence record.
LineageType.title: LineageType

Locator.description: Polymorphic locator into a source artifact. Describes HOW an anchor into a source artifact is shaped (character range, bounding box, sequence position, etc.), not WHAT kind of content the source contains. Discriminator is locator_type.

locator_type.description: Class name of the concrete Locator subclass (e.g. CharRangeLocator).

Locator.title: Locator

LocatorFidelity.description: The locator granularity an extraction profile expects on anchored content.
LocatorFidelity.title: LocatorFidelity

NegativeEvidenceRecord.description: First-class record of a search that returned no result under stated scope. Distinct from `unknown` (question not considered) and from `not_searched` (in scope but not attempted).

OperationalLayer.description: Abstract base for composable operational/interpretive layers that are not themselves viewpoint directives (ExtractionProfile, VocabularyPack, ReasoningPolicy). Inherits the full Object/Grit discipline. Reusable foundational layers set viewpoint_directive_id to the bootstrap meta-viewpoint (vpt:meta-v0).

RefusalState.description: Five-state refusal taxonomy. Distinct from EpistemicStatus — RefusalState answers "did we look, and what did we find?", EpistemicStatus answers "what kind of statement is this?".

ViewpointDirective.description: The interpretive frame under which grits are extracted. Itself an Object — a ViewpointDirective has its own viewpoint_directive_id (the bootstrap meta-viewpoint, or itself for the meta-viewpoint). Carries prompts, exemplars, vocabulary references, target schema, and the should_not_claim rules it imposes on extracted grits. Composable against parent viewpoint directives. Supported composition modes: additive, restrictive, overriding.
